response.justification: The candidate is an experienced web and full-stack engineer, but the profile is a poor match for this embedded networking security role. He lists no Japanese language ability (a hard requirement at native level), no meaningful C-for-UNIX or Linux kernel modification experience beyond a brief C/C++ stint in 2014-2015, and no exposure to L2/L3 switching, MACsec, 802.1X, or any of the listed security technologies. His depth is in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang for web platforms, which does not transfer to LAN-switch firmware development.
strengths: Long, continuous software-development track record (15+ years) far exceeding the 3-year minimum
Some low-level and systems exposure: C/C++ components at National Instruments, low-latency DLang services, Linux experience
Strong engineering-practice culture (TDD, CI/CD, testing tools) and demonstrated ability to learn new stacks
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed, failing a mandatory native-level requirement
No embedded networking, switch, or security-protocol experience (MACsec, 802.1X, SNMP/NETCONF, chip SDKs)
C for UNIX and Linux kernel source-modification experience is minimal and roughly a decade old
